title: comarostaphylis polifolia subsp. polifolia
intro: Comarostaphylis polifolia subsp. polifolia, also known as mountain cranberry or rock cranberry, is a woody shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Comarostaphylis polifolia subsp. polifolia include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
